characters.name: Dark Shadows (1966), Characters A-Z
characters.description: Characters are listed alphabetically by surname. In order to maintain wiki consistency any character appearing in an episode broadcast in 1966 is listed as being 1967. On-screen, the 1966 episodes, 1 to 135, occur over approximately the space of a month, and as such are grouped with the remainder of 1967. Also, events referred to on-screen as taking place in 1797, are listed here as 1796.

characters.name: Jane Ackerman
characters.description: Jane Ackermanwas a young woman that lived inCollinsport,Mainein the 1960s. One evening in1967, a mysterious stranger attacked Jane on the road leading into town. Jane managed to escape but was unable to identify her attacker. News of the attack spread quickly. WhenJoe Haskelllearned about it, he rushed to theCollinsport Innto tellMaggie Evanswhat had happened. 221(referenced only)

characters.description: Duane MorrisRobert Rodan 475 636

characters.name: Adam Collins
characters.description: Adam Collinswas the identityBarnabas Collinsmade up when he thought his life-force was going intoDr. Eric Lang'screation,Adam. "Adam" was to be one an english cousin of theCollins familyin order to pave the way for Barnabas' acceptance atCollinwood. Barnabas toldElizabeth Collins Stoddardthat he would be away for awhile and that he was expecting "Adam" Collins to pay him a visit very soon. When the experiment didn't go according to plan, and Barnabas remained in his original body, this new identity was no longer needed.

characters.name: Alice
characters.description: AlicewasBill Malloy's secretary at theCollins Fishing Fleet and Canneryin1967(9).

characters.name: Alice Chen
characters.description: Alice Chen was a nurse. She was working alongsideRichard HillatCollinsport Hospitalduring the events that led to thenear-destruction of the town.

characters.name: Andy
characters.description: Andy was a barman or waiter at theBlue Whale. A drunkenJoeonce called out to him (33).

characters.name: Anton Thompson
characters.description: Anton Thompsonwas a doctor who worked atWindcliff Sanitarium. Doctor Thompson greetedMaggie EvansandSebastian Shawupon their arrival after the events of1108. Doctor Thompson's body was discovered outside Windcliff by his colleague,Bernard Kear. He'd been murdered. It was later revealed that his killer was the newly-resurrectedEve.

characters.name: Sophie Baker
characters.description: Sophie Bakerwas a young woman living inCollinsportin the year1897. She claimed to have been a close associate of a "gentleman" namedCaptain Strathmore, as well as acquaintances with a drunkard namedCharlie.Barnabas Collinsencountered her near the fishing docks, and helped Sophie to locate her lost compact. Sophie took an immediate liking to Barnabas and asked him to escort her to theBlue Whale. Hisvampiricneed for blood began to quickly overtake him, and he tried to leave Sophie's company. While fixing her make-up, she noticed that Barnabas cast no reflection in her compact mirror. Unable to resist the blood-lust any longer, Barnabas attacked Sophie, killing her and drinking her blood.

characters.name: Oliver Bennett
characters.description: Oliver Bennettwas a cabinetmaker situated inMarion, Massachusettsduring1795. (mentioned only)

characters.name: Bernard Kear
characters.description: Bernard Kearwas a doctor who worked atWindcliff Sanitarium. Doctor Kear informedSebastian Shawof the death ofDoctor Laurie Norris. He also told him that he will be his new doctor. Moments after meeting Sebastian, he was shocked to discover the body of his colleague,Anton Thompson. Before he could get help, he was himself murdered by the newly-resurrectedEve.

characters.name: Bert Hackett
characters.description: Bert Hackettwas the name of theLeviathanchild's,Michael Hackett, made-up father. Mrs. Hutchins, a Leviathan follower, tellsJulia Hoffmanthat she knew Bert. (928).

characters.name: Nicholas Blair
characters.description: Humbert Allen AstredoColin Baker 521 979 "Every move unwise (toJeb Hawkes). And the most unwise of all, Barnabas Collins. You made him a vampire.  You had to make him completely useless to us! A shabby performance, indeed! And a dangerous one.  He will be after you, and he will be relentless about it."(953) Nicholas Blair tended to have a generalized, low opinion of humans, considering them petty mortals.  If they fought back he could be annoyed, or simply amused (the way a burglar would be amused by a small, furious dog). He scolded his sisterCassandrafor getting caught up in their affairs and disliked it when his own plans were put off-schedule or delayed by humans. When he executed Cassandra Blair, he set it up so she would face the fact that Barnabas Collins would never forgive her, so she could finally see the truth of their relationship for herself. When Angelique was resurrected as a vampire, he had some pity for her victim, Tom Jennings but didn't let it stop him from teaching her how to be an effective weapon.  Although Angelique had cursed Barnabas into his vampiric state, she had almost no knowledge of what it meant to be one; Blair guided her through the development of her unfamiliar powers, and praised her affectionately when she learned to draw on her own strength to compel Joe Haskell, instead of relying on him for aid. As the series progressed he developed a grudging respect for mortals.  Professor Stokes, he observed, must have considerable powers for a mortal. Dr.Julia Hoffmanwas the only human to have completely surprised him, because he knew she was very afraid of him yet confronted him anyway.  He called her "the inimitable Dr. Hoffman" and admitted he admired her. This did not stop him from enjoying the chance to terrify her when he found she was Jeb Hawkes' prisoner and though he had the chance to kill her, let her go with a warning to stay away from the Leviathans.(965). With Barnabas Collins he went from a blasé contempt for Angelique's obsession to warningJabez Hawkesthat Collins was 'relentless' as an opponent(953).  With Jeb Hawkes he demonstrated deep contempt for ruinous, selfish impulses that threatened the Leviathan cause. Carolyn Stoddard was initially of little note, but he treated her with oily charm, knowing she was essential to his plans for the Leviathans.

characters.name: Reverend Bland
characters.description: Reverend Bland(1722-1800s) was a minister who performed the wedding forBarnabas CollinsandAngelique Bouchard. He was also going to preside over Barnabas' marriage toJosette DuPres. He was married (397). 380,396,397

characters.name: Wilfred Block
characters.description: Wilfred "Fred" Blockwas a handyman and groundskeeper who worked for theCollins familyin1970parallel time, and spent most of his time working atLoomis House. He had a deep attraction toAlexis Stokes, and was killed byAngelique Stokes Collins, who had recently returned from the dead and was posing as Alexis. His death was necessary for Angelique, as she was forced to survive on the warmth of a living human. Block was played byEdmund Hashim. 1003

characters.name: Niall Bradford
characters.description: Niall Bradfordwas the name of the cousinBarnabas Collinssupposedly lived with inEnglandbefore coming toCollinsport. Barnabas mentions him when quizzed about his past byBurke Devlin(293), and one second time, in the Blue Whale (301). Burke sends private investigators out to check on Barnabas' story, revealing that there hasn't been a Niall Bradford living in London for 130 years (303). As the storyline progresses, the date of Barnabas' death will change from around1830to1795.

characters.name: Tim Braithwaite
characters.description: Tim Braithwaite(1807-1840) was an alcoholic sailor who lived in Collinsport in1840. Tim was played byDick Sabolfor one episode. In1840, Tim was hired byDesmond Collins, who was possessed byJudah Zacheryat the time, to help him remove the giant cross that was keeping Judah's coffin shut in a secret underground vault outside of Collinsport. While removing the cross, he found Judah's golden mask inside the coffin. When Tim finally got the cross off, he attempted to steal the golden mask, but Judah's headless body rose from the coffin and decapitated him. According to the originalQuentin Collins, Tim did not have a favorable reputation around town, and assumed no one would miss him once people found out he was dead.

characters.name: Tim Braithwaite (PT)
characters.description: John Kuhner as Tim Braithwaite Tim Braithwaite(d.1841) was a drinking companion ofGabriel Collinsin 1841 Parallel Time. He was paid $1,000 by Gabriel to spend a night in theLocked roomatCollinwoodin his place, because Gabriel was too afraid to do it himself. Gabriel spent roughly 30 minutes in the locked room before Tim arrived. WhenMorgan Collinsunlocked the room the following morning, he discovered that Gabriel was missing and Tim was dead. Morgan disposed of Tim's body, and found the check from Gabriel in the process.

characters.name: Reverend Brand
characters.description: Reverend Brandwas the minister who performed the wedding ceremony forCarolyn StoddardandJeb Hawkes. Elizabeth Collins Stoddard mentions that the Reverend Brand will be going from the ceremony to a "vestry meeting." Since the only major denomination in the United States in 1970 that used the word "vestry" to refer to its governing board was the Episcopal Church, it is likely that the Reverend Brand is supposed to be an Episcopal priest. [This is incorrect. There are also several different Anglican churches in the U.S. that have such meetings.] Reverend Brand was played byDonald Hotton.

characters.name: Reverend Brand
characters.description: Reverend Brandwas the minister who performed the wedding ceremony forCarolyn StoddardandJeb Hawkes. characters.name: Brett Hawker
characters.description: Brett Hawkerwas the caretaker atCollinwood, presumably employed some time after the death ofMatthew Morgan. He was killed byAlfie Chapman. Carolynasked Brett to check onGerald Conwaywho was staying atSeaview. Knocking on the door, he was unable to hear Gerald dying of a heart attack and being possessed by the spirit ofNicholas Blair. Brett metEmma Finneyoutside theCollins General Store. He flirted with her much to the annoyance of her boyfriend Alfie.

characters.name: David Brewster
characters.description: David Brewsterdrowned at sea and was buried atEagle Hill Cemetery. Thecaretakermentions him whenJoe Haskellasks if he's seenDavid Collins. 313(mentioned only)

characters.description: Clarice Blackburn 733 791

characters.description: Clarice Blackburn 733 791

characters.name: Simon Briar
characters.description: Simon Briarwas the brother ofMinerva Trask. She considered him the best man the world had ever known and after he died, she cried every night for over a year. She did not have proof, but she believed strongly that he was murdered byTim Shawwhile foiling a burglary.Rachel Drummondsaid the money was the wages they had earned, butReverend Gregory Traskand Minerva claimed that the money was to pay off their tuition when they were students atWorthington Hall(733). The true circumstances surrounding Simon's death are recounted in theBig Finishaudio dramaThe Crimson Pearl, where he was played byDavid Ames: Simon had stolen the titular pearl from where Gregory Trask had been hiding it (inside a hollowed-out Bible), but he was later surprised byAngelique, who killed him and took the pearl for her own purposes. Tim Shaw had come in just then, but Angelique used her powers to make him forget he had seen her.

characters.name: Andrew Bronson
characters.description: Andrew Bronsonand his family were victims ofJudah Zachery. (mentioned only)
